speaker: interviewer
text: Tape number 3, August the 27th, 1996. We are interviewing Abram Jachniuk. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] You were talking about running into somebody whom you knew [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] in the woods. When did this happen, and how old were you?

speaker: interviewee
text: I, I was about 16-- 16, maybe 16 and 1/2. I don't know exactly. It's something like that. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So we, we went to that-- we knew that we have somebody what-- his house is in the woods. And around him is nobody. We came to him. And we told him-- he, he saw us, knew-- they kill you. They start to kill already. They knew already that, sure. I said, help us, you know. He said, you know, that we are-- he knew our father. He knew us, you know. And if-- we'll be able to, to get-- you know, we knew-- we had a lot of things we still have. But, you know, if he'll be able to come to them, you know, we'll pay you off like that, what you're doing for us. He said, all right, OK, I'll, I'll hide you. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] He gave us to eat. And he took us. There was, like, a mountain of hay, you know. It was a mountain of hay. But he has-- in the hay, he has an empty place, like a hiding place. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So he shows us how go, go under there and go in. You know, it's-- nobody will find you here. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we were happy already that-- [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] so we were there. He, he bring us-- he came to us. He brought us food.

speaker: interviewee
text: And he told us stories that here, near me, three Jews they caught there. They shoot them. Four Jews, they caught-- that-- all around, you know. And they-- every day, they catching and they killing the Jews. And they were-- whoever catch, you know-- the Germans, the Ukrainians-- they kill the Jews. He told us good stories. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So next day, he-- so we, we knew that they would-- he told us. And probably it was true. A lot of that was true.

speaker: interviewee
text: But [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] next day in the morning, we hear somebody is, is going around the, the hay, you know. Somebody is going. We don't know.

speaker: interviewer
text: We were afraid, you know, they're looking for us. So we keep quiet. What else can we do?

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And then we, we hear, [CRYING] Yossel, Abram! You know, somebody is calling us. [CRYING] [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I had a brother. Monia came. He escaped from Gestapo. [CRYING] [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] So-- he escaped with one more-- [CRYING] with a friend, you know. They came. So we, we, we took them in, too.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where-- we had another brother, Aaron, there. Where-- what happened to Aaron? You see, the same night where they were killing-- took from the ghetto, they took from there. They were in Kostopol.

speaker: interviewee
text: From the ghetto, they took everybody near Kostopol there in the woods. And then they bring to the same-- the same place. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So I said, what happened to-- he told us that there was all men, you know. So they surrounded them. And they-- just the-- so a lot of the men start to run, you know.

speaker: interviewee
text: There was a-- water. You know, it was the-- a river, not, not too much water. So everybody-- you have to-- to escape, you have to go through that river.

speaker: interviewer
text: So a lot of people they kill in that river because in the water, you cannot-- so-- he said, I, I was running together with Aaron. [SOBBING] And probably, you know, that-- probably, they killed.

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we were-- that, that guy what he came with, with him, he was-- he was for a while-- Keep going. Keep going.

speaker: interviewee
text: He, he was-- he was for a while with, with us. And later, he said, I'll go-- I'll go to my-- he was from another village. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] I'll go to my-- I'll go to my village. So he went.

speaker: interviewer
text: So we were three brothers. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So we had something-- we thought-- and that Christian start already, knew-- when you were going to go out. But that's, that's already-- you know, it was a time-- it was a time that they used to kill a part.

speaker: interviewee
text: And then, you know, later, they said, you can go out. And people used to go out. And-- but this was already Juden free. We can-- whether they could, they used to kill for the, the last.

speaker: interviewer
text: So we had something. We gave it to him. And he was satisfied for, for a while. And later, he start to, you know, when? How long?

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we saw that we cannot trust him anymore. So we start-- we, we decide to escape, to run away from him. How long were you with this farmer?

speaker: interviewee
text: We, we were a short time, maybe, I don't know, maybe a week, maybe less than a week-- a short time because we, we were afraid. He didn't have a good reputation. He just-- he want, you know, he knew that we were not good. He didn't have a good reputation. So we were afraid to stay there anymore. In nighttime, we went in the woods. We knew there is partisans. We thought maybe we are going to find partisans. But we went in the woods. And we find three or four or more, more Jews from Korost. They escaped. So we were together with them [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] for a little while. And then, I don't remember how, but my brother Yossel find out there was one from our-- from Korost. He didn't go to the ghetto. He escaped right away in the-- in the woods. And we had-- we heard from his family that he is with the partisans. And we would like to, to find him. But took, took time, and we couldn't-- we couldn't find him. But one time, Yossel-- I don't know how he contact him-- and he decided to take us-- only asked two brothers. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] It-- that was another story what I like to-- like to return. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] When we were-- when we were in the-- already in the woods, they used to go to look for food. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So one, one night, my brother-- two brothers, Monia and Yossel, went to, to the Christians what-- they used to know them. And they used to come and say, can you help me with something? They used to give you a piece of bread and run. But this time, he said, you know what? [CRYING] [SOBBING] Aaron was yesterday-- Aaron, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] the fourth brother. He was-- he was looking for you. He asked maybe somebody escaped.

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we had already-- so he told them-- do as-- and he said that he going to come to him again. Aaron is going to come to him again-- if you'll know something about us. So we told him that if he'll come, keep him. And we'll, we'll return.

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So [SOBBING] this way, [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] we got together the brother. [CRYING] [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So we were already four brothers. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So then we were looking for partisans. But we couldn't find. But Yossel contact that-- it was [? Freisin ?] [? Rafael ?] [? Freisinger. ?] And he said that he can take us to the partisans.

speaker: interviewee
text: He told what place we can meet him. He showed my, my brother what place we can meet him. And he'll come with the-- with a few of the guys for the partisans, and we will get together. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we went there. And he said-- and, and it was a very-- woods. It was very crowded. It was very-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] it was very hard to go in that, that part. So-- but one time, we went there.

speaker: interviewee
text: He, he told them a signal. And they will come. You know, it's very hard to find. But he'll make like, a a bird, you know, a signal from a bird. So if you hear that signal, you know that we are coming. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we went. We find that place. And we were waiting for them. We were waiting for them. We wait. We wait.

speaker: interviewee
text: And they don't come. And it was a very dark night. It was so dark, you didn't-- couldn't see anything. And then we heard somebody is coming. And we heard shooting near, near the place. It was a little-- how do you call it, like, shack, you know, where we were waiting for them. And shooting around and running-- we didn't know what happened. Maybe, maybe Germans, they surrounded us.

speaker: interviewer
text: So we start-- we escaped from there, you know. We run away. And we were hiding, you know, not far from them.

speaker: interviewee
text: Later, later, in a-- maybe in a half an hour, we heard that signal, like a bird. So we knew that this must be them. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So they came. It was that [? Rafael ?] and two more guys. Those are from partisans. Were they Jewish partisans?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, they weren't Jewish. But, but that [? Rafael ?] was Jewish. And that there, I even don't know. But it was-- it was a very bad situation. It was a bad situation because they find out they have somebody in the partisans, a new guy. They took him in. And later, they find out that he is-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] he's danger, you know. He's-- he goes-- he robs Jewish wherever he find it. And beside that, he was danger for them. So that commander gave them a [NON-ENGLISH], how do you say, a order. We have to get rid of him. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So this was the order. They have to go with him. And they have to-- they have to shoot him-- partisans there.

speaker: interviewee
text: And they came. And they-- whenever they want to let him in front, he-- like he knew. He never go in the front, you know. He was hiding in the back. They want to let him in the front.

speaker: interviewer
text: So near that place where we supposed to-- they find the moment. And they tried to shoot him. Two people shoot him, and he escaped, you know. He run away. So they find only-- and he, he was wearing, I know a-- it was a, a Jewish-- it was a-- [NON-ENGLISH], you know. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] He was-- his coat was-- a bullet here. In the hat, was a bullet here. Well, they find the hat. And they find that coat. But he is not there. He disappeared. So what happened to him?

speaker: interviewee
text: You know, they were looking. Maybe they-- maybe he's somewhere in the-- you know, in the bushes. They looked. And they couldn't find him.

speaker: interviewer
text: So then they already-- it was-- they couldn't talk about us. It was, how are they going to come and tell, tell the, you know, commander that they, they couldn't-- didn't shoot him? He run away. Now they are in trouble. Who know-- he knows all the secrets, everything.

speaker: interviewee
text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So-- and they-- so they, they couldn't take us and said, we'll see you next time. See, it was already not-- they went. They have-- they used to have guys in the-- in the woods, where they used to take care-- used to go-- called [NON-ENGLISH], you know, guys in the-- in the woods.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the partisans knew all of them because they, they came to that-- they-- and said, you know what? Yesterday, [? Chapraz-- ?] they call him [? Chapraz-- ?] he came. He said he was a-- had a fight with Germans. And he was wounded, you know? So they knew already where he is. That was a big-- so then we-- well, that [? Rafael ?] told us that not far from that place is there a Polish-- a, a village, a Polish village. And there is Jewish people. And they-- the Jewish people are there.

speaker: interviewee
text: They don't touch them. They know that there is-- so we went to them. And we were there with them. There was-- we have about 35 people together.

speaker: interviewer
text: In what village?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was near Karaczun. The village was Karaczun. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So we, we stayed there for a while. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] We stay-- we stay for a while with-- in Karaczun with them. And the, the Polish people used to take us sometime to, to help them out to-- in the-- we were, you know, we feel better than-- you know, that we can. They used to give us food.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where did the other Jews come from?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was-- everybody escape. This is a-- they escaped from the ghetto. Some people escape from, from Brezno, some-- all around-- all around. And [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] so we were together. We were together there for a while. And later, [PAUSES FOR 11 SECONDS] one time, [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] it's, it's-- came a time where the Polish people-- with the Ukrainian, you know. The Nazis make-- the Polish, the Ukrainian became very big enemies. The Polacks used to kill the-- it was that if they caught a Ukrainian in-- in Polish, they used to kill him. It was very-- between them was a-- so-- and the Polish people used to ask us already to help them, to defend from the Ukrainian, you know. We have a few rifles. We had a few rifles with us. And they ask us. And it was so bad that they were afraid. It was-- a lot of Polish people were killed. It came to a point that they must run away, leave that village and run away. And they were very rich people there in Karaczun. So-- and they asked the Germans to give them-- and there was-- the railroad was near that. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So-- and the Germans give them-- give the wagons. And everybody was-- they left everything, whatever they could. And they went to Germany because they were afraid that the Ukrainian got there more-- they going to kill 'em. Because it was very bad. Sort of like with the Jews-- and they, they start with the Jews. And later between them was such a-- so for us was very bad because now the Ukrainian are going to come in. And we are afraid for them. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So we used to go-- we used to go nighttime in-- we used to look-- whatever we can find, food. Food-- we used to, in the beginning, I-- we used to go pick potatoes, you know. In the beginning, we were afraid.

speaker: interviewee
text: Several weeks, we didn't go nowhere. Only we used to go nighttime, pick potatoes. And we used to live there. Later, we start to go out, you know. They used to help us out, whatever, a piece of bread, you know.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where did you go to get the food?

speaker: interviewee
text: In, in the beginning, we used to go to-- in Korost, you know, in Korost-- used to go nighttime. It was very danger. But we used to go to people that we-- you know, the older people. They were-- the younger peoples, we were afraid for them. The other people was not bad. So-- and, and later, when the-- when the Polacks disappeared later, we didn't go already to Korost. We used to-- with the Karaczun, you know. I used to go over to Karaczun.

speaker: interviewer
text: So we used to-- they left a lot of things. So in the beginning, we make-- how you-- we prepare for us for a while so we can survive, you know. And we went-- that nobody can find us.

speaker: interviewee
text: But one time, a group of Ukrainians surrendered us. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] They surrounded us. And-- but most of us escaped, you know, because we have always for somebody watching, you know, if somebody is coming. And they let us know. We-- so they caught two my brothers, Yossel and Monia. And one, one lady with her daughter-- they got something-- about six people. And we were about 40 people all together, more or less. And they, they make a meeting between them. Some of them were watching the, the Jews where-- they caught. And the rest of the Ukraine, they make a meeting. And they come over. And they said, we are not killing any more Jews. We have a few Jews. They are working for us. And you don't have to be afraid. You don't have to hide anymore. So you can all together get together and come over to us. And we'll give you work. You don't have to--

speaker: interviewer
text: So my brother and another-- it was Shimon Bogart. He was a very-- he used to have always a rifle with him, very nice guy. He was very brave. So they decided to go. They said, come for-- the village was Kazimierz. Come to Kazimierz. There is our Stube. You'll see people are working in the-- come all together. So we decided not to go. We, we knew we can't trust them. But Yossel and Shimon said, my brother, let's go. Let's take a look. Maybe we'll see somebody on the way. We'll ask.

speaker: interviewee
text: And [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] when they-- when they went there, they, they met somebody. I remember exactly the way this is. Somebody came with a white horse, like [? Mosheh. ?] [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] It was somebody what they knew, a Christian.

speaker: interviewer
text: He said, where are you going? They told him the story. They told him what happened. They said, and they told us that there is-- they don't kill any more Jews. And we can come over there.

speaker: interviewee
text: He say, God forbid. There is no Jews. There is nobody. They want all of you there, you know. They have nobody. Run back. And wait another month, two-- the Russians are coming soon, you know. The front was coming there.

speaker: interviewer
text: So they came, and they told this story. And then-- but how do you hide from, you know, the Ukrainian? They know the woods like--

speaker: interviewee
text: What we decide-- there was-- there was a little railroad coming with it. The Germans used to go always patrol. So we decide-- and there was woods. But it was very short, you know. It was not big. So we decide that we'll go there. We'll go there because they will never think that the Jews went near the railroad because there are Germans going.

speaker: interviewer
text: So that's what we decide. We went there. And we were very quiet. You see, there-- I don't remember how long it took. And it was-- it was very-- we, we used to go out once in awhile to, to-- heard what's going on-- to, to Christians, what we knew them. And we saw the Russians are coming through the-- with the white-- how do you call, [NON-ENGLISH]? They, they first come with white clothes, you know.

speaker: interviewee
text: They're coming-- they, they came. And then we saw. They used-- we used to say, [? Mosheh ?] when they came in.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was [SIGHS] about January the 20th.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year?

speaker: interviewer
text: Let me take you back a little. What was the makeup of the group that you were with? Were there women, children?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was women. It was women, children. It was man. One, one was born in, you know, was born in the-- in the woods. A child was born. I remember-- and there was-- we were afraid, you know. You cannot cry. With a pillow, we used to cover the-- you know, when we start to cry. It was very hard, you know. It's very hard for the mother because, you know, you, you try. Nobody can, can hear you.
